person.name: Lady Alianore le Strange Baroness Grey of Ruthyn

nameForms.fullText: Lady Alianore le Strange Baroness Grey of Ruthyn

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Blackmere, and his wife Angharad Butler of Wem. (father shows "Blakemere, Weobley, Herefordshire" & his bro. Hamo shows "Blackmere, Crnwll")

She married Sir Reynold (or Reginald) de Grey, 2nd Lord Grey of Ruthin, around Oct 1352.

They had 4 sons and 2 daughters

1.) Sir Reynold, 3rd Baron

2.) John

3.) Edmund

4.) Roger, Rector of Blunham

5.) Isabel

6.) Ida, wife of John Cokayne

Eleanor died on 20 April 1396.

zh.Sedgebrook, Lincolnshire, England: 英國英格蘭林可爾史爾瑟其布雷可
zh.lifeSummary: Lady Alianore le Strange Baroness Grey of Ruthyn是Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere和Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer的女兒，於大約1328年出生在英國史洛普爾。她和Reynold Grey至少有3個兒子和4個女兒。她去世於1396年4月30日在英國威爾斯丹比爾盧享年69歲，埋葬在威爾士丹比爾盧。

zh-hans.Sedgebrook, Lincolnshire, England: 英国英格兰林可爾史爾瑟其布雷可
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lady Alianore le Strange Baroness Grey of Ruthyn是Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere和Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer的女儿，于大约1328年出生在英国史洛普爾。她和Reynold Grey至少有3个儿子和4个女儿。她去世于1396年4月30日在英国威尔斯丹比爾盧享年69岁，埋葬在威尔士丹比爾盧。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Strange
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Wiltshire and Gloucestershire): nickname for a newcomer to a district, from Middle English strange ‘foreign’ (Old French estrange, Latin extraneus, from extra ‘outside’).

Irish (Antrim): this name is also common in Ireland, where, in addition to its English etymology, it may represent an Anglicized form of any of several Irish names containing the element gall ‘stranger’, e.g. Doyle , Gallagher .

variants.name: Stranger
meanings: English: nickname for a newcomer to an area, from Middle English stra(u)nger(e) ‘stranger, foreigner’ (Old French estrangier). Compare Strange .

variants.name: Doyle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Sean, Kieran, Seamus, Aileen, Dermot, Liam, Niall, Brennan, Brigid, Conal, Conan, Donal.

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Dubhghaill ‘descendant of Dubhghall’, a personal name composed of the elements dubh ‘black’ + gall ‘stranger’. This was used as a byname for Scandinavians, in particular to distinguish the darker-haired Danes from fair-haired Norwegians. Compare McDougall , McDowell .

variants.name: Lestrange
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Strange 1 with fused Old French definite article le.

Irish: in Ireland, adopted for Mac Conchoigchríche ‘son of Cúchoigcriche’, a personal name meaning ‘hound of the borderland’. Lestrange was adopted for Mac Conchoigchríche probably due to the presence of a prominent family of Lestrange, of similar meaning, who settled in the counties of Westmeath and Offaly in the 16th century.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Lady Alianore le Strange Baroness Grey of Ruthyn was born about 1328, in Shropshire, England as the daughter of Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere and Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer. She had at least 3 sons and 4 daughters with Reynold Grey. She died on 30 April 1396, in Ruthin, Denbighshire, Wales, United Kingdom, at the age of 69, and was buried in Ruthin, Denbighshire, Wales.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Alianore le Strange Baroness Grey of Ruthyn est née environ 1328, à Shropshire, Angleterre, fille de Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere et Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles avec Reynold Grey. Elle est décédée le 30 avril 1396, à Ruthin, Denbighshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterrée à Ruthin, Denbighshire, Galles.

de.lifeSummary: Lady Alianore le Strange Baroness Grey of Ruthyn wurde um 1328 in Shropshire, England, als Tochter von Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere und Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer geboren. Sie hatte mit Reynold Grey mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 30. April 1396 im Alter von 69 Jahren in Ruthin, Denbighshire, Wales, United Kingdom und wurde in Ruthin, Denbighshire, Wales begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Lady Alianore le Strange Baroness Grey of Ruthyn è nata il circa 1328 nel Shropshire, England come figlia di Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere e Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie con Reynold Grey. Morì il 30 aprile 1396, nel Ruthin, Denbighshire, Galles, Regno Unito, all'età di 69 anni, e fu sepolta a Ruthin, Denbighshire, Galles.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Alianore le Strange Baroness Grey of Ruthynは約1328年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアでSir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of BlackmereとLady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmerの娘として生まれた。彼女はReynold Greyとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1396年4月30日にイギリスウェールズデンビグhシュアイアルスインで69歳で死去し，ウェールズデンビグhシュアイアルスインにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Alianore le Strange Baroness Grey of Ruthyn(은)는 대략 1328년 영국 슈럽실르에서 Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere(와)과 Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Reynold Grey(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1396년 4월 30일 영국 웨일스 덴비그h실르 루스인에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 웨일스 덴비그h실르 루스인에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Lady Alianore le Strange Baroness Grey of Ruthyn nasceu aproximadamente 1328, em Shropshire, Inglaterra filha de Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere e Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer. Ela teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas com Reynold Grey. Ela faleceu em 30 de abril de 1396, em Ruthin, Denbighshire, Gales, United Kingdom, com 69 anos, e foi sepultada em Ruthin, Denbighshire, Gales.

ru.lifeSummary: Lady Alianore le Strange Baroness Grey of Ruthyn родилась около 1328 г. в местности шропшир, Англия. Её родителей звали Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere и Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери от Reynold Grey. Она умерла 30 апреля 1396 г. в местности рутин, денбаишир, Уэльс, Великобритания в возрасте 69 лет и была похоронена в местности рутин, денбаишир, Уэльс.

es.lifeSummary: Lady Alianore le Strange Baroness Grey of Ruthyn nació aproximadamente 1328, en Shropshire, Inglaterra como hija de Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere y Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas con Reynold Grey. Murió el 30 de abril de 1396, en Ruthin, Denbighshire, Gales, Reino Unido, a la edad de 69 años, y fue sepultada en Ruthin, Denbighshire, Gales.
